signals.title: NZD/USD — G8 Bilateral Theme + Blowout NFP
signals.text: NZD/USD at 0.5803 — USD outperformance reaches critical levels following a blowout NFP report and rock-solid labor data, with markets now pricing slim odds of Fed cuts before 2028. The 150bp OIS carry advantage (Fed 3.75% vs RBNZ 2.25%) is reinforced by a massive G8 performance spread. NZD remains the G8 laggard as risk-off sentiment (SPX -1.81%) triggers a liquidation of high-beta positions. Watch for a break of 0.5800 to accelerate the move toward 0.5750.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Intervention Risk + Carry + CESI
signals.text: USD/JPY at 160.34 — price has breached the 160.00 psychological level, a zone previously associated with BoJ intervention. Despite the 288bp OIS carry advantage (Fed 3.75% vs BoJ 0.73%), 25d Risk Reversals show a -1.10 put skew, signaling the options market is paying for downside protection against a potential BoJ/MoF response. Strong JPY CESI (net +13) and wage growth headlines bolster the case for a BoJ hike on 16 Jun, creating a high-volatility ceiling at current levels.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Volatility + Retail Divergence
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7053 — COT LF net long +58,041 (76.1% long) represents a crowded institutional position facing a sharp reversal as SPX tumbles. Retail sentiment is 61% short, diverging from the institutional long bias. AUD/USD IV at 20.9% vs HV30 at 8.7% creates a massive +12.2% Vol Risk Premium, indicating extreme demand for downside hedging. The 60bp carry advantage (RBA 4.35% vs Fed 3.75%) is insufficient to offset risk-off liquidation. Watch for retail capitulation if 0.7000 is tested.

signals.title: USD/CAD — COT vs OIS Divergence + BoC Presser
signals.text: USD/CAD at 1.3944 — COT LF net short -42,194 (specs short) contradicts the 137bp OIS carry advantage favoring USD (Fed 3.75% vs BoC 2.24%). With the BoC on hold and Governor Macklem's press conference underway, the pair is testing recent highs. A sustained move above 1.4000 would likely trigger a short-squeeze of the crowded spec position. Real rate differential of +1.06% (USD +1.23% vs CAD +0.17%) structurally supports further upside.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Volatility Premium + Bear Steepener
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1526 — the pair is under pressure as the US yield curve shifts into a Bear Steepener (2s10s +56bp), favoring the USD through the term premium. IV at 7.1% vs HV30 at 4.3% (+2.8% VRP) shows the options market is pricing in higher volatility than recently realized. The 175bp carry deficit (Fed 3.75% vs ECB 2.0%) remains the primary structural drag. Watch the 11 Jun ECB meeting for any hawkish shift to counter the current USD momentum.
